mechanism_narrative: Sustained quarterly growth in real GDP accelerates corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as discount rates remain stable.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment drops below 5.5%, labor market tightness boosts consumer spending and wage growth, lifting equity valuations over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite falling unemployment.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and industrial production signals broad-based economic momentum, leading to positive SPX returns over a 60-day horizon as earnings revisions turn positive.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window when both series rise concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening cycle, rising policy rates combined with sticky inflation compress equity multiples, predicting negative SPX returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite rising rates and inflation.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month employment growth reinforces economic resilience, supporting positive SPX returns over a 5-day horizon as short-term risk premiums decline.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in employment growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating retail sales indicate strong consumer demand, driving positive SPX returns over a 20-day horizon as corporate revenue forecasts improve.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite accelerating retail sales.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production paired with accelerating inflation signals supply bottlenecks, leading to negative SPX returns over a 60-day horizon as margin compression and rate hike expectations weigh on valuations.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window when both series rise concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, sustained GDP growth supported by housing activity drives positive SPX returns over a 20-day horizon as earnings momentum peaks before potential slowdown.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite sustained GDP and housing growth.
